title: Scenario 07 External Authority Bridge Claim Boundary Contract
claim_supported: A Fork-preserved internal workflow record can be inspected by an external recipient.
The record preserves internal claim scope, non-claims, referenced evidence, and unresolved requirements.
The record can show that external authority conclusions were not established by the internal record.
claims_not_allowed_to_be_inferred: that a regulator, court, auditor, customer, insurer, or board must accept the record
non_inheritance_rule: No external authority-bearing property transfers merely because an internal Fork record is inspectable.

external_authority_boundary.internal_record_scope: record integrity, boundary state, non-claim preservation, reconstruction support
external_authority_boundary.external_authority_scope: admissibility, compliance, legal sufficiency, approval, acceptance, enforcement, coverage, or authorization

fork_non_authority_statement: Fork does not approve, certify, score, authorize, determine compliance, determine admissibility, establish legal sufficiency, decide acceptance, or judge correctness.
